Sariṣaba | Mahua | Parvapallī (Pabaulī) | Khaṇḍabalā 
| Gaṅgolī | Yamugāma | Kariyana | Mohara |

dirdhoṣa sub-epithet
śāṇḍilye dirdhoṣa: sarisaba, mahuā, parvapallī 
(pabāulī) khaṇḍabalā, gaṅgolī, yamugāma, kariyana,

sasārava: (sodarapura) stalita kaḍariyā, allāri, hoīyā, 
sameta talhanapura, parisarā, parasaṃḍā, vīranāma, 
uttamapura kodariyā, chatimana, barebā madhavāla,

iti śāṇḍilpaṃ

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
IAST ARCHIVE — B_7_Notes
Term / Field
Explanation
SHEET STRUCTURE
1_Marriage_Records
One row per dāu/ddāu transaction. Cross-ref to Sheet 
2 via Lineage_Chain_Ref (L-NNN). NEW: 
Marriage_Type, Incomplete_Flag.
2_Lineage_Records
One row per ancestor chain. Primary genealogical 
data. Keyed by Lineage_ID (L-NNN). NEW: 
Generation_Depth.
3_Name_Index
All named persons. With Role, Lineage_Ref, titles. 
NEW: Name_Type.
4_Village_Index
All villages with frequency, associated ancestors and 
brides. NEW: Village_Type.
5_Honorifics_Titles
All titles and scholarly epithets with Lineage_Ref.
6_Sections_Summary
Record counts per section including Complete vs 
Incomplete split.
NEW COLUMNS (v4)
Marriage_Type
primary = first/main dāu; secondary = ddāu 
(subsequent wife); dāuhitra = granddaughter given; 
unknown = type unclear
Incomplete_Flag
Y = bride name OR bride village is unknown (…); N = 
both bride name and village recorded. Enables filtering.
Generation_Depth
Integer: how many suto/suta steps are parsed within 
this lineage record. 1 = single step; 2+ = multi-
generation chain visible in one record.
Name_Type
personal = standard personal name; alias (pro) = name 
introduced by pro (also known as); epithet = 
literary/pen name; title+name = honorific title fused 
with name; compound = two or more names listed 
together
Village_Type
grāma = single village name; compound = two place 
names joined (e.g. Sihāuli Māṇḍara, Tripurāuli 
Narauna, Rajāurā Māṇḍara); unknown = not 
determinable
KEY ABBREVIATIONS
suto / suta / sutāu
IPA suto / sutā / sutāu — son / sons of
sṃ / saṃ
IPA saṃ — from (village of origin particle)
pro / pra.
IPA prao — also known as / alias
dāu
primary marriage (daughter given)
ddāu
secondary / subsequent wife
dāuhitra
granddaughter given in marriage
apara / aparā
another / alternate branch of lineage
dario
also (secondary listing marker)
pāṃ / pāṃ
honorific prefix (IPA pāṃ)
vāvu
respectful address / honorific prefix
muśarī
learned / honorific title preceding name
ṭhākura
noble / landholder title
rājā
royal / noble address
u.t. / uo ṭīo
Upa-Tīpa cross-reference

records.text:
ś. / śāo
Śālivāhana era cross-reference
m.m.u. / mahāmaho
Mahāmahopādhyāya (highest scholarly title)
maho
Mahopādhyāya
mīmāṃsaka
scholar of Mīmāṃsā philosophy
bhāṣāśāstrī
scholar of linguistics / grammar
bahu bhāṣāvid
polyglot — many-language scholar
ROW COLOURS
White / light blue alternating
Normal complete records
Yellow (Sheet 1)
Incomplete record — bride name and/or village 
unknown

Kumāra Kamalānanda Siṃha: Śikṣāmantri (Education 
Minister) + Kulapati (University Rector) + Hindū 
Mahāsabhā Adhyakṣa + Svarājapārṭī Sadasyō — 4 
political/institutional roles
Tāntrika title (§34)
Tāntrika Vāvu Nityānanda Siṃha — unique; indicates 
tantric religious practitioner

Sārasavakavi Kulapati = Poet Laureate + Rector; 
'Sārasava' = Sarasvatī (goddess of learning); prestigious 
combined title

Appears as bride-father in §26 (L-341), §28 (L-383), 
§32 (L-536 ref) — Karamahā family; mō mō = informal 
mahāmaho
Kavi Bholana (recurring)
Recurring bride-father from Pavāuli (§2, §22 §33) — 
noted Maithili poet

§30 has 38 marriage records — largest single section in

§26 Lakṣamaṇa Miśra

§40 fully incomplete
Entire §40 section has '...' ellipsis for all bride entries —

§41 Jīveśvara cluster
Three scholarly sons: Jyotirvida Bhavanātha 
(astronomer), Vāiyākaraṇa Śyāmanātha (grammarian), 
Vaiyā. Maho Śrīnātha (grammarian + Mahopādhyāya)

Aśokacandra, Dineśacandra, Amareśacandra — 
systematic -candra suffix convention (parallel to -
ambara in §19–§21)

Bheśa suta Kulapati / Mānika / Sukavi — sukavi = 
skilled/accomplished poet; embedded as personal 
epithet in lineage entry

M.M.Ḍā. Gaṃgānātha = Mahāmahopādhyāya + 
Ḍākṭara (Doctor) — rarest combined title in entire 
khaṇḍa: highest traditional + modern academic

4 consecutive Maho (Mahopādhyāya) lineages: Maho 
Śobhanātha, Maho Jagadīśa, Maho Nandīnātha, Maho

cluster in Lālagañja

§79 M.M.U. Sacala pra. Bhavānīdatta

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
§82 mahārāja chain
Mahārāja Mādhava Siṃha is son of Mahārāja Chatra 
Siṃha — two-generation mahārāja chain; only such 
father–son noble pair in the khaṇḍa
§83 13-descendant triple cluster
Three ancestor chains in §83 each produce 5 sons — 
13 named descendants across Miśra Jayadeva, Vābu 
Muralī pra. Girijānātha, Citranātha
§84 four-alias sons
Miśra Bholā pra. Tulānātha's 4 sons each carry a pra. 
alias: Lakṣmīnātha, Rāma pra. Ḍīhanātha, Lakṣamaṇa 
pra. Ḍīmbhanātha, Bharata pra. Balabhadrā — 
systematic alias naming
§93 -dhara suffix cluster
Gaṃgādhara's 4 sons: Cirañjīva pra. Hemādhara, 
Mādhava pra. Dharmadhara, Ānanda pra. Modadhara, 
Jagadīśa pra. Ḍīmbadhara — -dhara suffix with pra. 
alias each
§97 three Vaiyākaraṇa sons
Vaiyā. Cuntī pra. Śrīdhara's sons: Vaiyā. Baladeva, 
Paraśurāma, Balabhadrā — father + at least one son 
both Vāiyākaraṇa
§98 Ṭhākura cluster
4 Ṭhākura family members: Ṭha. Mārkaṇḍeya, Ṭha. 
Rameśa, Ṭha. Kamalākānta, Ṭha. Rāmakṛṣṇa — 
Nagavāḍa Ghosāuta noble family
§100 Mahāmaho Rāmeśvara
Rucāuli Karamahā grāmārambha; Mahāmaho title at 
section opening — scholarly grāma cluster
Kavi Bholana (recurring)
Appears as bride-father §2, §22, §33, §100, §102 — 5 
sections across the khaṇḍa; most recurring non-
ancestor figure; Pavāuli Kavi
§78 Jyo. Soṇamaṇi
Jamdāulī Pāli; Jyotirvida (astronomer) title; son named 
Kavi Lālajhaḍūlākā — unusual double hereditary 
literary/scholarly tradition

§1–§103 of ~§224 total

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
NOTABLE FEATURES §104–§132
§109 Rājā Ṭaṃkanātha
Major noble lineage: Rājā Ṭaṃkanātha → 3 Kumāra 
sons (Karmanātha, Śeṣanātha, Rudranātha); grandson 
Proḥ. Girīndranātha = Professor. Unique 3-generation 
progression from rājā to kumāra to professor.
§111 -mādhava suffix
Govardvana's 4 sons: Kṛṣṇamādhava, Veṇīmādhava, 
Sūryamādhava, Candramādhava — systematic -
mādhava compound naming convention (parallel to -
ambara §19–§21, -candra §44)
§116 Caṃcalā pra. Somadatta
5-son cluster in §116: Dāmodara, Jhiṃgura, Mahādeva, 
Devana, Gudurā — dense lineage
§117 Ācārya Śivaśaṃkara
Ācārya title = religious/scholarly teacher; Bholā (§117) 
→ Mārkaṇḍeya → Bholāka → Ācārya Śivaśaṃkara — 3-
gen scholarly progression
§124 M.B.S.S. Civil Surgeon
Badrīnātha §124 has 'M.B.S.S. Civil Surgeon' notation 
— unique modern medical title; earliest medical 
professional title in khaṇḍa
§131 Kavi Bholana (6th appearance)
Kavi Bholana now confirmed in 6 sections: §2, §22, 
§33, §100, §102, §131 — most recurring non-ancestor 
figure across entire khaṇḍa
§109 Maladvāra Sṭeṭa
'Maladvāra Sṭeṭa' = Maldāhā Railway Station area — 
one of very few references to modern infrastructure in 
the pañjī
§129 Cilakā cross-reference
§129 Sarisava sṃ Cilakā explicitly cross-references the 
§13 Cilakā chain (L-156) — rare intra-khaṇḍa explicit 
cross-reference
CUMULATIVE TOTALS
Total Marriage_Records

Total Lineage_Records
84 (B7) + 740 (B1–B6) = 824 total, L-01 to L-824
Sections covered
§1–§132 of §224 total

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
NOTABLE FEATURES §133–§165
§133 Four-degree cluster
Satīśvara's 4 sons: I.A.S. Maṃtreśvara (Indian 
Administrative Service) + M.B.B.S. Yaṃtreśvara 
(Medicine) + Proḥ. Svatanṃtreśvara (Professor) + L.L.B. 
Bhaktiśvara (Law) — unique 4-sibling modern degree 
cluster in entire khaṇḍa
§133 Double M.M.U.
Vācā pra. Sābheśvara → M.M.U. Phaṇīśvara + M.M.U. 
Maṇīśvara — two Mahāmahopādhyāya brothers; only 
such sibling pair in khaṇḍa
§134 Vidyā Vācaspati
Vidhyā Vācaspati Vaiyā. Śaśinātha — Vidyā Vācaspati = 
D.Litt. equivalent traditional award; combined with 
Vāiyākaraṇa title; rare compound
§142 Vaidika + Kavi siblings
Nakaṭū pra. Viṣṇudaya → Vaidika Candradaya + Kavi 
Umādattā — Vedic scholar and poet as siblings; unique 
multi-tradition family
§155 -śaṃkara cluster
Nidhinatha's 6 sons: Śivaṃśakara, Hariśaṃkara, 
Dayāśaṃkara, Ramāśaṃkara, Śambhuśaṃkara, 
Vijayāśaṃkara — largest suffix-naming cluster in 
khaṇḍa (surpasses §111 -mādhava 4-son, §19–§21 -
ambara 4-son)
§156 7-son cluster
Khaganātha → 7 sons: Śūlapaṇi, Gadāpaṇi, 
Khaḍugapaṇi, Yādava, Ṭhīṭhara, Devakīnanda, 
Garībānanda — largest single-generation cluster in 
khaṇḍa
§158 COLOPHON
Unique: pañjī compiler names himself: 'iti Pāṃ 
Vidyānanda suputrasya Abhinava Murārī Pāṭhādi 
Ratāulī Darihara Upa-Tīpa Āṣāḍha Sapamī Budha 
09.07.2008' — son of Pāṃ Vidyānanda; Ratāulī 
Darihara; date = Āṣāḍha 7, Wednesday, 09 July 2008 CE
§165 Nāyāyika
Nāyāyika Maho Durgādhara — Nāyāyika = expert in 
Nyāya logic / Navya-Nyāya tradition; directly relevant 
to the scholarly lineage of Gaṃgeśa Upādhyāya of 
Mithilā
§149 Sadupādhyāya cross-reference
Sadupādhyāya Ānanda §149 chain directly parallels L-
100–L-101 from §8–§9; confirms multi-branch pañjī 
cross-referencing system
§160 -nandana cluster
Dharmanandana's 5 sons: Śambhunandana, 
Śaktinandana, Hirānandana, Ratananandana, 
Śailanandana — 5th such suffix cluster identified
CUMULATIVE TOTALS
Total Marriage_Records
122 (B8) + 1,122 (B1–B7) = 1,244 total, 02-001 to 02-
1044
Total Lineage_Records
122 (B8) + 824 (B1–B7) = 946 total, L-01 to L-946
Sections covered
§1–§165 of §224 total

§175 Vaidika + Jyotirvida siblings
Medhāpaṭi's sons: Vaidika Acchapaṭi (Vedic scholar) + 
Jyo. Bataḥā Kā (astronomer) — Vedic and astronomical 
traditions co-occurring in same sibship
§183 M.M.U. Sadde pra. Sadānanda
Sulhanī Narauṇa grāmārambha; M.M.U. with 6 sons 
including Maho Pā. Baṭeśa — M.M.U. lineage 
continuing
§204 Sāhityakāra Proḥ. Tantranātha
Sāhityakāra = literary critic/scholar; combined with 
Professor; Kaviśeśvara Badrīnātha bride-father — most 
specialized literary-academic cluster
§205 Samājaśāstrī Proḥ. Ugranātha
Samājaśāstrī = sociologist/social scientist; only such 
title in entire khaṇḍa 2; reflects 20th-century 
disciplinary specialization
§208 Rāja Cilakā multi-Rājā cluster
Rāja Cilakā pra. Sidda Candranārāyaṇa with 4+ Rājā 
sons (Devanārāyaṇa, Śrī Nārāyaṇa, Śyāmanārāyaṇa, 
Lalīṭa Nārāyaṇa) — cross-ref §28/§128 Cilakā chain
§212 Lt. Colonel Guṇākara
Son of Ḍā. Yogānanda; Lt. Colonel = Lieutenant Colonel 
military rank — unique in entire khaṇḍa; only military 
officer rank recorded
§218 Dharmadhurīṇa M.M.
Dharmadhurīṇa Mahāmahopādhyāya Kṛṣṇa Siṃha — 
Dharmadhurīṇa = upholder of dharma (unique title); 
combined with M.M. — rarest compound title in 
§215–§222 cluster
§222 M.Ku. Kiśora Siṃha
M.Ku. = Mahā Kumāra; son of Mahārāja Rudra Siṃha 
§222 — only Mahā Kumāra title in khaṇḍa
Rājagrama Ṭhākura cluster §215–§221 7 sections of Bhāura sṃ Rājagrama contain dense 
Ṭhākura family: Ṭha. Dīvanātha Siṃha, Ṭha. Jaya 
Siṃha, Ṭha. Rāmeśvara Siṃha, Ṭha. Harikṛṣṇa, Ṭha. 
Dullaha, Ṭha. Giradhara, Ṭha. Śrīdhara, Ṭha. 
Anūpanātha

records.text:

Bacce pra. Nandīpaṭi §179 → Bavejī pra. Śrīpaṭi → Rājā

§35: Kumāra Kamalānanda Siṃha — Śikṣāmantri +

Sadasyō
Only pañjī compiler named
§158 COLOPHON: Pāṃ Vidyānanda suputrasya 
Abhinava Murārī, 09.07.2008

§212: Lt. Colonel Guṇākara — only military officer in 
khaṇḍa

§205: Samājaśāstrī Proḥ. Ugranātha — only sociologist 
in khaṇḍa
Only Mahā Kumāra title
§222: M.Ku. Kiśora Siṃha
Deepest multi-gen chain
§22: 
Vadana→Laccī→Sugāka→Candramaṇi→Harṣamaṇi 
(4–5 generations)
